title_en: Great Treatise on the Five Constant Governances
paragraphs_en: Huangdi asked: The great void is vast and empty; the five movements circulate and press upon one another, waning and waxing unequally, diminishing and increasing in mutual succession. I wish to hear how the balanced qi is named, and how it is classified. Qibo replied: How illuminating is this question! Wood, in balance, is called Spreading Harmony; fire is called Ascending Brightness; earth is called Comprehensive Transformation; metal is called Discerning Balance; water is called Tranquil Compliance. Huangdi said: What of when they are insufficient? Qibo said: Wood, when insufficient, is called Diminished Harmony; fire is called Suppressed Brightness; earth is called Debased Oversight; metal is called Compliant Change; water is called Dried-up Flow. Huangdi said: What is meant by excess? Qibo said: Wood, when excessive, is called Bursting Forth Life; fire is called Blazing Brilliance; earth is called Abundant Elevation; metal is called Firm Completion; water is called Overflowing Abundance. Huangdi said: I wish to hear the signs of these three levels of qi in their categories. Qibo said: How thorough is this question! In the category of Spreading Harmony, the virtue of wood circulates everywhere; yang unfolds and yin spreads, and the five transformations are proclaimed in balance. Its qi is upright; its nature is compliant; its function is bending and straightening; its transformation is engendering flourishing growth; its class is grasses and trees; its governance is outward release; its climatic sign is mild warmth; its seasonal command is wind; its viscus is the liver — the liver fears clear coolness; its ruling orifice is the eye; its grain is hemp; its fruit is the plum; its seed-body is the pit; its correspondence is spring; its creatures are the furred kind; its domestic animal is the dog; its color is blue-green; its nourishment is the sinews; its disease is urgent tension and distension of the flanks; its flavor is sour; its note is jue; its substance is hard at the core; its number is eight. In the category of Ascending Brightness, true yang governs; its virtue is bestowed everywhere and universally, and the five transformations are evenly balanced. Its qi is lofty; its nature is swift; its function is scorching and blazing; its transformation is luxuriant flourishing; its class is fire; its governance is bright radiance; its climatic sign is blazing heat; its seasonal command is heat; its viscus is the heart — the heart fears cold; its ruling orifice is the tongue; its grain is wheat; its fruit is the apricot; its seed-body is net-veined; its correspondence is summer; its creatures are the feathered kind; its domestic animal is the horse; its color is red; its nourishment is the blood; its disease is twitching and spasm; its flavor is bitter; its note is zhi; its substance is the pulse-vessel; its number is seven. In the category of Comprehensive Transformation, its qi harmonizes with heaven's blessing; its virtue flows through the governance of all four others, and the five transformations are equally cultivated. Its qi is level; its nature is compliant; its function acts on the high and the low alike; its transformation is fullness and abundance; its class is earth; its governance is calm stillness; its climatic sign is moist, steaming heat; its seasonal command is dampness; its viscus is the spleen — the spleen fears wind; its ruling orifice is the mouth; its grain is millet; its fruit is the jujube; its seed-body is flesh; its correspondence is late summer; its creatures are the bare-skinned kind; its domestic animal is the ox; its color is yellow; its nourishment is the flesh; its disease is blockage and fullness; its flavor is sweet; its note is gong; its substance is the flesh; its number is five. In the category of Discerning Balance, it gathers in without contention and kills without transgression; the five transformations are proclaimed clearly. Its qi is pure; its nature is firm; its function is scattering and shedding; its transformation is hardening and constraining; its class is metal; its governance is forceful sternness; its climatic sign is clear sharpness; its seasonal command is dryness; its viscus is the lung — the lung fears heat; its ruling orifice is the nose; its grain is rice; its fruit is the peach; its seed-body is the husk; its correspondence is autumn; its creatures are the shelled kind; its domestic animal is the fowl; its color is white; its nourishment is the skin and body hair; its disease is coughing; its flavor is pungent; its note is shang; its substance is hard on the outside; its number is nine. In the category of Tranquil Compliance, it stores away without causing harm, and governs by favoring descent; the five transformations are all put in order. Its qi is clear; its nature is downward; its function is to irrigate abundantly; its transformation is congealing and hardening; its class is water; its governance is flowing and spreading; its climatic sign is congealing sternness; its seasonal command is cold; its viscus is the kidney — the kidney fears dampness; its ruling orifice is the two lower orifices; its grain is beans; its fruit is the chestnut; its seed-body is moist; its correspondence is winter; its creatures are the scaled kind; its domestic animal is the pig; its color is black; its nourishment is the bones and marrow; its disease is reversal; its flavor is salty; its note is yu; its substance is moist; its number is six. Therefore: engender life without killing it; foster growth without punishing it; transform without controlling it; gather in without harming it; store away without suppressing it — this is called the balanced qi.

In the category of Diminished Harmony, this is called 'the overcoming of engendering.' The qi of engendering fails to govern, so the transforming qi of earth rises up; the qi of growth settles into its own balance, while the command of harvesting arrives early. Cool rains fall in their season; wind and clouds arise together. Grasses and trees flourish late, their blue-green stalks wither and shed; yet things that bloom still bear fruit, the skin and flesh filling within. Its qi is constrained; its function is gathering inward; its pathological movement is cramping, twisting, and slackened contraction; its outbreak brings fright and alarm. Its viscus is the liver; its fruits are the jujube and plum; its seed-bodies are pits and husks; its grains are millet and rice; its flavors are sour and pungent; its colors are white and blue-green; its domestic animals are the dog and fowl; its creatures are the furred and shelled kinds; its characteristic weather is fog, dew, and bleak chill; its notes are jue and shang; its disease is trembling, unsteadiness, and anxious fear, following the transformation of metal. When the minor jue coincides with the intercalated shang, when the ascending jue matches the true jue, and when the ascending shang matches the true shang, its disease is limb paralysis, welling-abscess, swelling, and sores, with sweet-craving parasites — evil has injured the liver. When the ascending gong matches the true gong, and withering desolation sets in, then blazing heat surges and boils over; the calamity falls at the third number — this is what is called the 'revenge.' Its governing creatures are flying insects, wood-borers, maggots, and pheasants, and it manifests as thunder and lightning. In the category of Suppressed Brightness, this is called 'the overcoming of growth.' The qi of growth fails to spread; the storing qi of water instead prevails; the harvesting qi governs on its own; the transforming command achieves balance. Cold and clear chill arise repeatedly; the command of summer heat grows thin. Sustained by transformation, things are engendered yet fail to grow; they reach completion while still immature, and by the time transformation arrives they have already aged. Yang qi is bent and suppressed; hibernating creatures retreat into dormancy early. Its qi is stagnant; its function is sudden and violent; its pathological movement alternates between manifest and hidden, ever-changing; its outbreak brings pain. Its viscus is the heart; its fruits are the chestnut and peach; its seed-bodies are net-veined and moist; its grains are beans and rice; its flavors are bitter and salty; its colors are black and vermilion; its domestic animals are the horse and pig; its creatures are the feathered and scaled kinds; its characteristic weather is ice, snow, frost, and cold; its notes are zhi and yu; its disease is confusion, delusion, sorrow, and forgetfulness, following the transformation of water. When the minor zhi matches the minor yu, and the ascending shang matches the true shang, evil has injured the heart. When congealing bleakness and biting cold prevail, torrential rains and prolonged downpours follow; the calamity falls at the ninth number; its governing signs are sudden cloudbursts, startling thunder and lightning, and deep overcast with excessive rain. In the category of Debased Oversight, this is called 'diminished transformation.' The transforming qi fails to issue its command; the governance of engendering alone flourishes; the qi of growth is orderly, the rains fall irregularly, and the harvesting qi is balanced. Wind and cold arise together; grasses and trees flourish beautifully, blooming but not bearing fruit, and what they complete is mere chaff. Its qi is dispersed; its function is quiet stillness; its pathological movement is ulceration, welling swelling, splitting, and abscess-collapse; its outbreak is dampness and stagnation. Its viscus is the spleen; its fruits are the plum and chestnut; its seed-bodies are moist and pitted; its grains are beans and hemp; its flavors are sour and sweet; its colors are blue-green and yellow; its domestic animals are the ox and dog; its creatures are the bare-skinned and furred kinds; its characteristic weather is whirling gusts and violent gales; its notes are gong and jue; its disease is retention, fullness, and blockage, following the transformation of wood. When the minor gong matches the minor jue, when the ascending gong matches the true gong, and the ascending jue matches the true jue, its disease is undigested-food diarrhea — evil has injured the spleen. When shaking gales and whirling winds prevail, the blue-green foliage withers and scatters; its calamity falls at the four cardinal corners; its governing sign is destruction by tigers and wolves; clear metal qi then takes effect, and the governance of engendering is humbled. In the category of Compliant Change, this is called 'broken harvest.' The harvesting qi falls behind; the engendering qi rises up; the qi of growth and transformation combine their virtues; the governance of fire is proclaimed, and the multitude of creatures thereby flourish. Its qi is expansive; its function is agitated and urgent; its pathological movement is clanging spasm, blockage, and fainting reversal; its outbreak is coughing and panting. Its viscus is the lung; its fruits are the plum and apricot; its seed-bodies are husk and net-fiber; its grains are hemp and wheat; its flavors are bitter and pungent; its colors are white and vermilion; its domestic animals are the fowl and sheep; its creatures are the shelled and feathered kinds; its characteristic weather is bright radiance and searing heat; its notes are shang and zhi; its disease is sneezing, coughing, and nasal congestion with bleeding, following the transformation of fire. When the minor shang matches the minor zhi, when the ascending shang matches the true shang, and the ascending jue matches the true jue, evil has injured the lung. When blazing brilliance grows fierce and intense, ice, snow, frost, and hail follow; the calamity falls at the seventh number; its governing creatures are the scaled and hibernating kinds, pigs and rats. The seasonal qi arrives early, and great cold is thereby engendered. In the category of Dried-up Flow, this is called 'the reversal of yang.' The storing command fails to be raised; the transforming qi of earth flourishes; the growth qi is proclaimed and spread; hibernating creatures fail to retreat into dormancy. The soil is moist yet the springs diminish; grasses and trees grow luxuriant in their branches, blooming in full abundance. Its qi is stagnant; its function is seeping and draining; its pathological movement is hardening and blockage; its outbreak is dryness and desiccation. Its viscus is the kidney; its fruits are the jujube and apricot; its seed-bodies are moist flesh; its grains are broomcorn and millet; its flavors are sweet and salty; its colors are yellow and black; its domestic animals are the pig and ox; its creatures are the scaled and bare-skinned kinds; its characteristic weather is murky dust and gloom-shrouded haze; its notes are yu and gong; its disease is wilting, reversal, and hardened stool, following the transformation of earth. When the minor yu matches the minor gong, and the ascending gong matches the true gong, its disease is urinary blockage — evil has injured the kidney. When dust-laden gloom brings sudden downpours, shaking, uprooting gales follow; the calamity falls at the first number; its governing creatures are conspicuous furred ones, foxes and raccoon-dogs. Change occurs without proper storage; thus the counter-qi rides upon the vulnerable moment and moves, arriving unsummoned, violent and tyrannical, devoid of virtue, and disaster in turn befalls it. When the deficiency is slight, the revenge is slight; when severe, the revenge is severe — this is the constancy of qi.

In the category of Bursting Forth Life, this is called 'opening and unfurling.' The soil is loosened and drained; the blue-green qi of wood reaches through; harmonious yang spreads its transforming influence, and yin qi follows along. The qi of engendering is pure and transforming, and the myriad things thereby flourish. Its transformation is engendering; its qi is lush; its governance is dispersal; its command is unfolding and stretching; its pathological movement is dizziness, vertigo, and disorders of the vertex; its virtue manifests as rustling wind and the opening and splitting of buds; its calamitous change is shaking, uprooting, destructive gales. Its grains are hemp and rice; its domestic animals are the fowl and dog; its fruits are the plum and peach; its colors are blue-green, yellow, and white; its flavors are sour, sweet, and pungent; its season-symbol is spring; its channels are the foot jueyin and shaoyang; its viscera are the liver and spleen; its creatures are the furred and shelled kinds; its substances are hard at the core and hard on the outside; its disease is anger. When the greater jue matches the ascending shang, and when the ascending zhi prevails so that its qi turns rebellious, its disease is vomiting and diarrhea. If wood fails to attend to its virtue, then the harvesting qi of metal takes revenge: the autumn qi grows forceful and keen, and in severe cases turns to killing severity; clear metal qi arrives in full force, grasses and trees wither and fall, and evil then injures the liver. In the category of Blazing Brilliance, this is called 'luxuriant flourishing.' Yin qi transforms within, yang qi flourishes without; blazing summer heat carries out its transformation, and things thereby prosper. Its transformation is growth; its qi is lofty; its governance is movement; its command is resounding and manifest; its pathological movement is scorching heat and reckless disturbance; its virtue manifests as warmth, heat, and steaming humidity; its calamitous change is fierce blazing and seething boil-over. Its grains are wheat and beans; its domestic animals are the sheep and pig; its fruits are the apricot and chestnut; its colors are red, white, and black; its flavors are bitter, pungent, and salty; its season-symbol is summer; its channels are the hand shaoyin and taiyang, and the hand jueyin and shaoyang; its viscera are the heart and lung; its creatures are the feathered and scaled kinds; its substances are the pulse-vessel and moist flesh; its disease is manic laughter, malarial disorders, sores and ulcers, blood flowing freely, wild delirium, and reddened eyes. When the ascending yu matches the true zhi, its harvest phase proceeds evenly, yet its disease is tetanic rigidity, for when the ascending zhi prevails the harvesting qi of metal lags behind. When its governance grows violently intense, the storing qi of water takes revenge, and congealing bleakness appears out of season; in severe cases rain, frost, and hail bring biting cold, and evil injures the heart. In the category of Abundant Elevation, this is called 'broad transformation.' Its generous virtue is pure and still; it follows growth so as to become full; the utmost yin is replete within, and things are transformed to fullness and completion. Smoke-like haze and dust hang thick and murky, appearing over the deep soil; heavy rains fall in season; damp qi thereby takes effect, and the governance of dryness is held in check. Its transformation is fullness; its qi is abundant; its governance is stillness; its command is all-encompassing; its pathological movement is dampness accumulating and pooling; its virtue manifests as softness, moistness, and heavy sodden wetness; its calamitous change is shock, sudden gales, and collapse. Its grains are millet and hemp; its domestic animals are the ox and dog; its fruits are the jujube and plum; its colors are yellow, black, and blue-green; its flavors are sweet, salty, and sour; its season-symbol is late summer; its channels are the foot taiyin and yangming; its viscera are the spleen and kidney; its creatures are the bare-skinned and furred kinds; its substances are the flesh and the pit; its disease is abdominal fullness and inability to raise the four limbs. When great winds arrive suddenly, evil injures the spleen. In the category of Firm Completion, this is called 'gathering and contracting.' Heaven's qi is pure; earth's qi is bright; yang qi follows along; yin governs the transformation; dryness carries out its governance, and things are thereby brought to completion. The harvesting qi spreads abundantly, yet the transformation of growth does not reach its end. Its transformation is completion; its qi is pared and sharp; its governance is stern; its command is keen and cutting; its pathological movement is sudden fractures, sores, and chronic ailments; its virtue manifests as fog, dew, and rustling wind; its calamitous change is killing severity and withering desolation. Its grains are rice and broomcorn millet; its domestic animals are the fowl and horse; its fruits are the peach and apricot; its colors are white, blue-green, and vermilion; its flavors are pungent, sour, and bitter; its season-symbol is autumn; its channels are the hand taiyin and yangming; its viscera are the lung and liver; its creatures are the shelled and feathered kinds; its substances are shell and net-fiber; its disease is panting, wheezing, chest oppression, and having to sit upright to breathe. When the ascending zhi matches the true shang, its engendering phase proceeds evenly, yet its disease is coughing. When its governance changes violently, even the great trees fail to flourish and the tender, brittle growth scorches at the tip; the growth qi of fire then comes to the rescue — a great fire spreads, searing heat arrives, and the vines will wither; evil injures the lung. In the category of Overflowing Abundance, this is called 'sealing and storing.' Cold governs the transformation of things; heaven and earth are stern and frozen; the governance of storage spreads abroad, and the command of growth fails to be raised. Its transformation is bitter cold; its qi is hard; its governance is quiet stillness; its command is flowing and pouring; its pathological movement is drifting discharge, drenching, and welling up; its virtue manifests as congealing bleakness and cold mist; its calamitous change is ice, snow, frost, and hail. Its grains are beans and millet; its domestic animals are the pig and ox; its fruits are the chestnut and jujube; its colors are black, vermilion, and yellow; its flavors are salty, bitter, and sweet; its season-symbol is winter; its channels are the foot shaoyin and taiyang; its viscera are the kidney and heart; its creatures are the scaled and bare-skinned kinds; its substances are moist and full; its disease is distension. When the ascending yu prevails, the growth qi of fire fails to transform. When its governance goes to excess, the transforming qi of earth rises up greatly and dust-laden haze fills the exchange of qi; heavy rains fall in season, and evil injures the kidney. Hence it is said: when a phase fails to maintain its constant virtue, the one it overcomes comes to take revenge; when its governance constantly upholds its proper order, the one it overcomes is transformed in accordance with it. This is what is meant.

Huangdi said: Heaven is insufficient in the northwest, where the left is cold and the right is cool; earth is not full in the southeast, where the right is hot and the left is warm. What is the reason for this? Qibo said: This is a matter of yin and yang qi, of the principle of high and low, and of the difference between the great and the lesser. The southeast is yang, and yang's essence descends downward, so the right is hot and the left is warm there. The northwest is yin, and yin's essence is offered upward, so the left is cold and the right is cool there. Thus the land has its heights and depths, and the qi has its warmth and coolness: where the land is high, the qi is cold; where the land is low, the qi is hot. Therefore those who dwell amid cold and coolness develop distension, and those who dwell amid warmth and heat develop sores; purging relieves the distension, and inducing sweat relieves the sores. This is the constant principle of the opening and closing of the striae and pores, and merely reflects the difference between the great and the lesser degrees of yin and yang. Huangdi said: How does this bear upon longevity and early death? Qibo said: Where yin essence is offered upward, people live long; where yang essence descends, people die young. Huangdi said: Good. When people there fall ill, how should they be treated? Qibo said: For the qi of the northwest, disperse it and cool it; for the qi of the southeast, gather it in and warm it. This is what is meant by 'the same disease treated differently.' Hence it is said: where the qi is cold or cool, treat with cold and cooling methods, applying water to soak the body; where the qi is warm or hot, treat with warming and heating methods, strengthening what is guarded within. One must match the treatment to the local qi, and balance can thereby be achieved. In false, counterfeit presentations, do the reverse.

Huangdi said: Good. Within a single region, the qi's engendering and transformation, and people's longevity and early death, differ — what is the reason? Qibo said: This follows the principle of high and low; it is caused by the lay of the land. Where the land is lofty, yin qi governs it; where the land is low-lying, yang qi governs it. Where yang prevails, things mature before their allotted time; where yin prevails, things mature after their time. This is the constant principle of terrestrial pattern, and the way of engendering and transformation. Huangdi said: Does this also govern longevity and early death? Qibo said: Where the land is high, its qi favors long life; where the land is low, its qi favors early death — and this varies with the size of the land, small differences in elevation producing small differences, great differences producing great differences. Therefore one who treats disease must clearly understand the way of heaven and the pattern of earth, the alternating prevalence of yin and yang, the precedence and sequence of qi, the longevity and early death of people, and the timing of engendering and transformation — only then can one truly know a person's bodily form and qi.

Huangdi said: Good. In certain years, people do not fall ill, yet the qi of a viscus fails to respond or to function as expected — why is this? Qibo said: Heaven's qi controls it; the viscus qi has something it follows. Huangdi said: I wish to hear this in full. Qibo said: When shaoyang governs heaven, fire qi descends and presides below, and lung qi rises to follow it; whiteness arises and metal takes effect, while grasses and trees suffer calamity. Fire appears and scorches, subduing and consuming metal; great heat then prevails, causing coughing, sneezing, nasal congestion with bleeding, and stuffed nose; there are sores, alternating cold and heat, and swelling of the flesh. Wind moves over the earth, dust and sand fly up; there is heart pain and stomach-duct pain, reversal-counterflow, and blockage of the diaphragm. Its onset is sudden and rapid. When yangming governs heaven, dryness qi descends and presides below, and liver qi rises to follow it; blue-green arises and the function of wood is established, while earth suffers calamity. Bleak chill arrives repeatedly, wood is felled and grasses wither; there is rib-side pain, reddened eyes, trembling and shivering with chattering teeth, and sinew wilting that prevents long standing. Sudden heat arrives, earth then grows hot; yang qi is pent up and bursts forth as pain; urination changes; there is alternating cold and heat resembling malarial disorder, and in severe cases heart pain. Fire spreads over dried grass, flowing water does not freeze, and hibernating creatures appear out of season. When taiyang governs heaven, cold qi descends and presides below, and heart qi rises to follow it, so that fire also grows bright; vermilion arises, and metal thereby suffers calamity. Cold and clear chill arise from time to time, and when they prevail, water freezes. Fire qi is lofty and bright: there is heat in the heart, vexation, dryness of the throat, frequent thirst, sneezing and nasal congestion, tendency to sorrow, and frequent yawning. When heat qi moves recklessly, cold then takes revenge: frost falls out of season, there is forgetfulness, and in severe cases heart pain. The soil becomes moist, water grows abundant, cold invades, and deep yin transforms into dampness; the qi changes and things become water and fluid retained internally — there is fullness in the middle and inability to eat, numbness of the skin and heaviness of the flesh, and impaired function of the sinews and vessels; in severe cases there is swelling of the instep and abscess on the back of the body. When jueyin governs heaven, wind qi descends and presides below, and spleen qi rises to follow it, so that earth also grows abundant; yellow arises, and water thereby suffers calamity. The function of earth is altered: there is heaviness of the body, wilting of the muscles and flesh, reduced appetite, and blandness in the mouth. Wind sweeps through the great void, clouds and objects sway and shake, the eyes spin and the ears ring. Fire indulges its violence, the earth then grows hot, great heat consumes and sears, red discharge flows downward, hibernating creatures appear repeatedly, flowing water does not freeze — its onset is swift and sudden. When shaoyin governs heaven, heat qi descends and presides below, and lung qi rises to follow it; whiteness arises and metal takes effect, while grasses and trees suffer calamity. There is panting, vomiting, alternating cold and heat, sneezing, nasal congestion with bleeding, and stuffed nose; great heat prevails widely, and in severe cases there are burning sores and ulcers, heat so fierce as to melt metal and liquefy stone. The earth then grows dry and clear, bleak chill arrives repeatedly; there is rib-side pain and frequent sighing, killing severity prevails, and grasses and trees are transformed. When taiyin governs heaven, damp qi descends and presides below, and kidney qi rises to follow it; blackness arises and water is transformed; dust-laden haze, clouds, and rain appear; there is discomfort in the chest, impotence, and the qi is greatly weakened so that it cannot rise and cannot function. At such times there is, on the contrary, pain in the lower back and buttocks, and difficulty in movement and turning. There is cold-limb reversal; the earth then stores yin, great cold is about to arrive, and hibernating creatures retreat early. There is oppressive pain below the heart; the earth cracks and ice hardens; there is pain in the lower abdomen that at times interferes with eating. When water overrides metal, the stored water increases, the flavor that dominates is then salty, and the flow of water diminishes.

Huangdi said: In certain years, pregnancies fail to come to term, and treatment does not reach completion — what qi causes this? Qibo said: Among the six qi and the five categories of creatures, there is mutual overcoming and control: those that share the same phase as the governing qi flourish, while those that differ decline. This is the way of heaven and earth, the constancy of engendering and transformation. Thus, when jueyin governs heaven, the furred creatures are quiescent, the feathered creatures thrive, and the shelled creatures fail to reach completion; when jueyin governs the earth below, the furred creatures thrive, the bare-skinned creatures are depleted, and the feathered creatures fail to reproduce. When shaoyin governs heaven, the feathered creatures are quiescent, the shelled creatures thrive, and the furred creatures fail to reach completion; when shaoyin governs the earth below, the feathered creatures thrive, and the shelled creatures are depleted and fail to reproduce. When taiyin governs heaven, the bare-skinned creatures are quiescent, the scaled creatures thrive, and the feathered creatures fail to reach completion; when taiyin governs the earth below, the bare-skinned creatures thrive, and the scaled creatures fail to reach completion. When shaoyang governs heaven, the feathered creatures are quiescent, the furred creatures thrive, and the bare-skinned creatures fail to reach completion; when shaoyang governs the earth below, the feathered creatures thrive, the shelled creatures are depleted, and the furred creatures fail to reproduce. When yangming governs heaven, the shelled creatures are quiescent, the feathered creatures thrive, and the shelled creatures fail to reach completion; when yangming governs the earth below, the shelled creatures thrive, the furred creatures are depleted, and the feathered creatures fail to reach completion. When taiyang governs heaven, the scaled creatures are quiescent and the bare-skinned creatures thrive; when taiyang governs the earth below, the scaled creatures are depleted and the bare-skinned creatures fail to reproduce. In every case, when a creature-category is overridden during the movement in which it fails to reach completion, its failure is severe. Thus the governing qi has that which it controls, and the established year has that which it generates; earth's qi controls what it overcomes, and heaven's qi controls what overcomes it. Heaven governs color, earth governs form; the decline and flourishing of the five categories each follow what is suitable to their qi. Hence there are years when pregnancies fail to come to term and treatment does not reach completion — this is the constancy of qi, what is called 'rooted within.' What is rooted without is likewise fivefold; thus the distinctions of engendering and transformation comprise five qi, five flavors, five colors, five categories, and five suitabilities. Huangdi said: What does this mean? Qibo said: What is rooted within is called the pivot of spirit — when spirit departs, the pivot ceases. What is rooted without is called the establishment of qi — when qi stops, transformation is severed. Thus each has its controls, each has its overcoming, each has its engendering, and each has its completion. Hence it is said: one who does not understand what influence the year adds, and the sameness or difference of qi, is not fit to speak of engendering and transformation. This is what is meant.

Huangdi said: When qi begins, engendering and transformation occur; when qi disperses, form comes into being; when qi spreads, multiplication and nurture occur; when qi ends, the outward image changes — in this, all things arrive at the same principle. And yet, in what the five flavors provide, engendering and transformation may be thin or thick, and maturation may be scant or abundant, so that the end and beginning differ — what is the reason for this? Qibo said: It is controlled by earth's qi. It is not that heaven fails to engender — it is that earth fails to foster growth uniformly. Huangdi said: I wish to hear this principle. Qibo said: Cold, heat, dryness, and dampness produce different transformations. Thus, when shaoyang governs the earth below, cold-toxin fails to arise; its flavor is pungent; it is treated with bitter and sour; its grains are blue-green and vermilion. When yangming governs the earth below, damp-toxin fails to arise; its flavor is sour; its qi is damp; it is treated with pungent, bitter, and sweet; its grains are vermilion and white. When taiyang governs the earth below, heat-toxin fails to arise; its flavor is bitter; it is treated with bland and salty; its grains are yellow and black. When jueyin governs the earth below, clear-cold-toxin fails to arise; its flavor is sweet; it is treated with sour and bitter; its grains are blue-green and red; its qi is concentrated and its flavor is pure. When shaoyin governs the earth below, cold-toxin fails to arise; its flavor is pungent; it is treated with pungent, bitter, and sweet; its grains are white and vermilion. When taiyin governs the earth below, dryness-toxin fails to arise; its flavor is salty; its qi is hot; it is treated with sweet and salty; its grains are yellow and black. When transformation is pure, the salty flavor holds firm; when qi is concentrated, the pungent flavor transforms and both are brought into balance.

Hence it is said: in supplementing the upper or lower body, follow along with the qi; in treating the upper or lower, go against the qi — regulate according to the location and the prevalence or decline of cold and heat. Hence it is said: take the treatment from above or from below, from within or from without, in order to seek out the fault. Those who can tolerate toxicity are treated with potent medicines; those who cannot withstand toxicity are treated with mild medicines. This is what is meant. When the qi is contrary to the location of disease: if the disease is above, treat from below; if the disease is below, treat from above; if the disease is in the middle, treat from the sides. Treat heat with cold, but administer it warm; treat cold with heat, but administer it cool; treat warmth with clearing coolness, but administer it cold; treat clear coolness with warmth, but administer it hot. Thus, whether dissolving or paring away, inducing vomiting or purging, supplementing or draining, the same method applies whether the disease is chronic or recent. Huangdi said: When disease is in the middle, yet is neither excess nor hard, now gathering and now dispersing, what then? Qibo said: How thorough is this question! Where there is no fixed accumulation, seek out the affected viscus; if it is deficient, supplement it — expel the pathogen with medicine, follow up with diet, and apply water-soaking therapy; harmonize the interior and exterior, and the disease can be brought to complete resolution. Huangdi said: For medicines with toxicity and without toxicity, is there a rule for their administration? Qibo said: Disease may be chronic or recent, prescriptions may be large or small, and medicines may be toxic or non-toxic — there is indeed a constant rule to be observed. When treating disease with highly toxic medicine, stop when six-tenths of the pathogen is removed; with ordinarily toxic medicine, stop at seven-tenths removed; with mildly toxic medicine, stop at eight-tenths removed; with non-toxic medicine, stop at nine-tenths removed. Thereafter, grains, meats, fruits, and vegetables should be used to nourish and complete the recovery — do not overdo it, lest one injure the correct qi. If the disease is not yet eliminated, resume treatment according to the same method. One must always take the year's qi into account first, and never assault the harmony of heaven; do not further fill what is already full, do not further deplete what is already deficient, thereby bringing calamity upon a person from heaven itself; do not induce evil qi, and do not lose the correct qi — these errors would cut short a person's long life. Huangdi said: In those with chronic illness, whose qi, though following treatment, does not regain health, and who remain emaciated even after the disease has left — what then? Qibo said: How illuminating is this question of the sage! Transformation cannot be replaced by artificial means, and the proper season cannot be defied. Once the channels and network vessels are opened and the blood and qi made compliant, restore what is insufficient until it is equal with the rest of the body; nourish it and harmonize it, remain still and await the proper time, carefully guard the qi and do not let it be displaced — then the bodily form will become manifest and the qi of life will grow and flourish. This is called the way of the sage-king. Hence the Great Essentials say: do not replace transformation, do not defy the season; one must nourish, one must harmonize, and await its natural return. This is what is meant. Huangdi said: Good.
